rulings.Case Title: PERFORMING ARTS LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. MICHAEL MURRAY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Leo Cassidy As An Individual And As Trustee Of The Leo & Deidre Cassidy Family Trust, James Nunemacher To Compel Responses To Discovery From Christopher Flood
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, June 24, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANTs JAMES NUNEMACHAER and THE CHRIS JAMES CORPORATION DBA VAN GUARD's Motion O To Compel Responses To Discovery From Christopher Flood. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion by Seller Defendants to compel discovery responses from Cross-Defendant, Christopher Flood, is granted. Cross-defendant shall provide verified responses, without objection, to Judicial Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Requests for Production of Documents and Requests for Admissions. Cross defendant shall respond to said discovery within 30 days from the date of service of this Order with Notice of Entry. The Seller Defendants motion that Requests for Admissions be deemed admitted is denied. The Cross defendant served his verified Response to Admissions prior to the date of the hearing on this motion, which responses minimally but adequately complied with CCP 2033.220. However, pursuant to CCP 2033.280(c) the imposition of monetary sanctions is mandatory. Sanctions are awarded in favor of the moving party as against Christopher Flood in the sum of $500.00 to be paid within thirty days. Further sanctions were not considered based on Cross Defendant's failure to respond timely to the served discovery as said moving party failed to comply with the notice requirement of CCP 2023.040. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: UN PLAZA LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. UN PLAZA ASSOCIATES, LP A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Oiton For Cost Recovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 24, 2014, Line 14, DEFENDANT UN PLAZA ASSOCIATES, L.P., HILL-UN PLAZA ASSOCIATES, INC.'S Motion For Cost Recovery. Denied, without prejudice. Defendant's motion is premature pending a final disposition of the issues raised in Defendants' unresolved cross-complaint. (Day v. Papadakis (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 503, 513, disapproved on another ground in Morehart v. County of Santa Barbara (1994) 7 Cal.4th 725.) "The language authorizing determination of who is the prevailing party "whether or not the suit proceeds to final judgment" (Civ. Code, § 1717, subd. (b)(1)) does not imply that the suit need not be finally disposed of . . . ." (Bank of Idaho v. Pine Avenue Associates (1982) 137 Cal.App.3d 5, 15 disapproved on another ground in Snyder v. Marcus & Millichap (1996) 46 Cal.App.4th 1099.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: VALLEY INTERNATIONAL ACADEMY VS. AMERICAN L & L EDUCATION, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Costs And Expenses, Including Attorneys' Fees Against The Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, June 24, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF VALLEY INTERNATIONAL ACADEMY's Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Costs And Expenses, Including Attorneys' Fees. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel responses to Special Interrogatories fully and without objection is granted and was unopposed. The defendant failed to confirm in an opposition the responses were in fact timely served on November 18, 2014. Therefore the parties' written agreement dated March 27, 2014 to provide responses without objection is operative. The defendants shall provide their further responses within 14 court days following service of this order with Notice of Entry. The plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions is granted as against defendants' counsel in the amount of $860 to be paid within thirty days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 24, 2014, Line 15, DEFENDANTS HAROLD WONG AND WILLIAM YEE AND ASSOCIATES' Motion To Compel 1) Substantive Response To Special Interrogatories, Set One 2) Substantive Responses To Request For Production Set One 3) Request For Sanctions. Within 10 days, Plaintiff Lem Sek Wong shall provide verified responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One, nos. 9-20, 22-40, 46-48, 51-54, 60-63, 68-75, 81, 104-105, 108-110, 132-134 and 145 and Requests for Production, Set One, nos. 25 - 59 and produce responsive documents. Monetary sanctions of $ 2,400.00 are awarded in favor of Defendants Harold Wong and William Yee & Associates and against Plaintiff Lem Sek Wong to be paid within 30 days of service of notice of entry of order. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. rulings.Case Title: VINCENT CONSTRUCTION GROUP, A GEORGIA CORPORATION et al VS. GOLDEN BEAR INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 24, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANTS DIGIACOMO INSURANCE CO., LAWRENCE DIGIACOMO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled as to the breach of oral contract cause of action. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the negligent misrepresentation cause of action for plaintiff to allege the claim with more particularity. See Charnay v. Cobert (2006) 145 Cal.App.4th 170, 185, fn. 14. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the declaratory relief cause of action. See CCP 1061. The claim is not necessary as plaintiff's other claims can address the issues raised by the declaratory relief cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: VINCENT CONSTRUCTION GROUP, A GEORGIA CORPORATION et al VS. GOLDEN BEAR INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 24, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANTS DIGIACOMO INSURANCE CO., LAWRENCE DIGIACOMO'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The motion to strike is granted and denied in part. The motion is granted without leave to amend as to the emotional distress allegations that are alleged against the moving defendants. The motion is denied as to the general damages allegations. The motion is denied as to the claim for attorney's fees. The complaint is seeking those fees as damages under the tort of another doctrine. The motion is denied as to the punitive damages allegations because plaintiff does not seek that relief from defendant. rulings.Case Title: JUAN DESOTO VS. WAHEED KHUGIANI et al*****TRANSFERRED TO SOLANO COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Change Of Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 24, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANTS WAHEED KHUGIANI, FERRO AUTOMOTIVE GROUP INC., GENOA AUTOMOTIVE INC.'S Motion For Change Of Venue. Granted. (CCP § 395.5.) Venue is proper in Solano County. Defendants' principal place of business is in Fairfield, California, which is located in Solano County. (Holvick Decl., Exhibits A and B.) The meeting minutes are not dispositive. There is no evidence that the San Francisco address was ever submitted to the Secretary of State as the principal place of business. The address of the agent for service of process is not relevant.
